title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Hercule Poirot discusses the death of Rosalind Claude with Inspector Spence, noting that she was murdered rather than committing suicide. Poirot reveals that a call was made from a public telephone booth at Wormsley Vicarage to the Sheepfold apartment at 10:15 PM on Tuesday, implying someone was present in the flat. He argues that the woman seen in an orange scarf was not Rosalind Claude, which shifts the motive and identity of the killer. Poirot classifies the three deaths in the case differently: he views the death of Enoch Dorrington (Charles Trenton) as an accident where no one intended to kill him, one death as a suicide, and Rosalind Claude's death as a cold, premeditated murder. He suggests there are two distinct murderers. Poirot leaves Spence to wait for a letter from overseas.

Poirot then visits Madame Claude to question her about her husband’s morphine addiction, which she explains began during the war due to overwork and neuralgia. He asks if she met anyone at the public telephone booth on the night she called Lynn Marchmont. Madame Claude recalls borrowing two pence from a woman exiting the booth. She describes the woman as looking like an actress wearing an orange scarf, noting that the woman's face seemed familiar, as if she were someone who had already passed away. Meanwhile, Lynn Marchmont decides she must go to the Longrein Residence to speak with Rolly face-to-face, feeling that a storm is approaching and that she is about to say goodbye to her current way of life.
